summary: Iteration 175: Verified unified legal document formatting across all case detail tabs. All 9 tabs (Documents, Timeline, Grounds, Notes, Reports, Legal, Case Law, Progress, Collaboration) now use Times New Roman serif font via the .legal-content CSS class. Buttons and badges correctly remain sans-serif (Manrope).
critical_code_review_comments: All 9 case detail tabs have legal-content class applied in CaseDetail.jsx TabsContent wrappers
CSS .legal-content class in index.css correctly sets font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif
Body text uses 12pt Times New Roman as specified
Headings (h1-h6) use Times New Roman with bold weight and appropriate sizes
Buttons remain sans-serif (Manrope) - correctly excluded from legal-content styling
Tab triggers remain sans-serif (Manrope) - correctly excluded from legal-content styling
Case identity card uses Times New Roman for defendant, offence, state, sentence fields
No console errors found during testing
Navigation between all tabs works correctly
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 175 verified unified legal document formatting. All case detail tabs now use Times New Roman font via .legal-content CSS class. Test case used: case_ac67738895f9 (R v Marika). Key findings: (1) All 9 tabs have legal-content class with Times New Roman font, (2) Buttons/badges correctly use Manrope sans-serif, (3) H1 uses 36px/800 weight Times New Roman, (4) Body text uses 12px Times New Roman, (5) Ground titles use 16px/700 weight Times New Roman, (6) No console errors, (7) Tab navigation works correctly.
